reasoning: The qwen model identified a critical risk in picorv32.sh that downloads+executes unverified code during build. This violates AUR security practices and introduces supply-chain risks. Deepseek missed this file entirely.
disagreement_analysis: Deepseek reviewed different files (CMake configs) while qwen found the risky build script. Coverage gap in deepseek's analysis caused disagreement.
coverage_issues: deepseek failed to review src/nextpnr/ice40/picorv32.sh - the critical unsafe component
false_negatives: deepseek missed picorv32.sh's unsafe download+execution pattern
Both models missed verifying if PKGBUILD actually runs picorv32.sh (needs build process analysis)
re_audit_focus: Verify if picorv32.sh is executed during build via PKGBUILD functions
Review all ice40/ scripts for similar patterns
learnings: File selection must prioritize build-related scripts (especially .sh/.py in src subdirs)
Models should cross-reference PKGBUILD functions to identify executed files
Improve risk detection for build-time network access patterns (wget/curl in scripts)
